checks.a_analysis: The output contains no fabricated body text or headings. The metadata title and image alt texts are within the scope of acceptable AI-generated additions for accessibility.
The phone number and physical address in the output match the source exactly. No fictitious URLs, phone numbers, or email addresses were added.

The document is a single-page infographic. The output captures the primary heading and a portion of the contact info, and represents the rest as images. No entire pages are missing, and the main heading structure is preserved.
The source document does not contain any formal tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The source document consists primarily of a detailed flowchart outlining seven steps with multiple sub-tasks and decision logic. The output JSON fails to extract any of the text content from these flowchart boxes into structured text elements (paragraphs or lists). This results in the loss of nearly 90% of the document's informative text content, which is only partially summarized in the image alt text.
checks.b_evidence_source: All text content within the flowchart boxes (Steps 1-7), including lists like '1. Identifies & provides Signatory Authority', '2. Identifies internal points of contact', '1. Meet with Agency NCID Administrator', '1. Assist Agency with adding Org/Div/Sect', etc.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text content for these steps is completely missing from the 'elements' array.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main body section, following the contact information paragraph (index 2).

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The primary contact email address found in the source document is omitted from the corresponding paragraph in the output.
checks.b_evidence_output: Primary Contact: CJLEADS 3514 Bush Street Raleigh, NC 27609 919-754-6949

checks.a_analysis: I have carefully compared the source document and the output JSON. The text in the output JSON, including the heading and the primary contact information, appears only once and is not duplicated. The images identified at the end of the document represent distinct graphical elements from the source. There is no evidence of paragraphs or sections appearing multiple times.

The output JSON correctly identifies 'How Does My Agency Get CJLEADS?' as a Level 1 heading, which corresponds to the main title in the source PDF. There are no other headings in the source or output, so the hierarchy is flat but correct and logical for the structure of this single-page document.
The source document contains a flowchart consisting of various boxes and arrows, but it does not contain any formal data tables. Therefore, there is no table structure to be corrupted in the output.

The reading order in the JSON (Title -> Contact Info -> Logo Image -> Flowchart Image) follows a logical top-to-bottom sequence. While the 'Primary Contact' information is visually located at the bottom left of the source PDF, moving it before the graphical elements in the conversion is an acceptable reordering for accessibility purposes. The flow of information remains logical and faithful to the intent of the document.

The source document contains several structured lists within the flowchart (Step 2 has a 5-point numbered list, Step 3 has lists in both decision branches, and Step 5 has a 2-point numbered list). The output JSON fails to capture these as structural list elements (e.g., 'type': 'list'), instead capturing the entire flowchart as an image with a text summary. This is a failure to preserve the structural organization of lists present in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Numbered list starting with: '1. Identifies & provides Signatory Authority' in Step 2 box of the flowchart.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON does not contain any 'list' type elements; all list content from the source is omitted or summarized in image alt text.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains a contact email address 'cjleadshelp@nc.gov' in the bottom left corner. This link is completely missing from the output JSON, which only includes the mailing address and phone number in the 'Primary Contact' paragraph. This is a missing link defect for a government contact URL.
checks.b_evidence_output: The paragraph at reading_order 2 only contains: 'Primary Contact: CJLEADS 3514 Bush Street Raleigh, NC 27609 919-754-6949'

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully recreates the visual layout of the source PDF. The primary content of the PDF is a complex flowchart, which is rendered in the HTML as a high-fidelity image or graphic that preserves all boxes, colors, arrows, and spatial relationships. While the contact information has been extracted and placed at the top of the HTML for better accessibility and readability in a responsive format, the core organization and visual flow of the information match the source.
There are no tables in the source PDF document. The content consists of a flowchart diagram and text blocks, which are handled correctly in the conversion.

All meaningful visual elements from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the CJLEADS logo (seals and NC map), the entire 7-step flowchart diagram, and all associated icons. The flowchart is presented as a clear, high-resolution graphic with a corresponding textual description for accessibility.

There is no duplication of body content. While the document title and primary contact information appear both as plain text at the top of the HTML and within the graphic rendering of the flowchart, this is a standard and expected practice for accessible document conversion to ensure key information is machine-readable and properly indexed (WCAG guidelines). No paragraphs of body text or instructions are duplicated in a way that would confuse a user.
